content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine correctness or information-loss bugs in the prompt-budget system. The stale omission count (d279826d) is a clear off-by-N display bug with a secondary byte-budget inaccuracy. The silent planned-elsewhere drop (6de2affb) can produce a materially false 'none supplied' message. The all-or-nothing dependency collapse (d50af35f) can eliminate every concrete dependency detail. Each warrants a fix.

## Votes

- `fr-17-d279826d`: Accept
  Verified in service.rs:942-958. `omission_line` is computed once at line 947 using the omitted count before the pop-loop. Each `selected.pop()` increases the true omitted count but never recomputes the text. The displayed count will under-report omissions (e.g. showing '15 omitted' when 17 are actually omitted after 2 pops). The secondary effect is also real: the stale (shorter) text passes the byte budget check, but the correct (longer) text could push the output over `DEPENDENCY_CONTEXT_MAX_BYTES`. Recomputing after each pop is the correct fix.
- `fr-17-6de2affb`: Accept
  Verified in project.rs:1556-1598. `fit_planned_elsewhere_item_to_budget` returns `None` when the base line (id + title + relationship + status) exceeds `remaining_bytes`. The caller at line 1541 uses `continue`, silently skipping the item. If every item exceeds the budget this way — plausible with long bead titles or relationship strings — `selected` is returned empty, and `format_planned_elsewhere` at service.rs:961-963 emits the 'No explicit planned-elsewhere items were supplied' message. This is a genuine information-loss path that produces a materially false statement to the agent. A truncated base line or omission marker would preserve the signal that planned-elsewhere items exist.
- `fr-17-d50af35f`: Accept
  Verified in service.rs:907-958. The selection loop (lines 922-940) adds items one at a time, checking the cumulative byte count including an omission line. If the very first rendered dependency item plus its omission line exceeds `DEPENDENCY_CONTEXT_MAX_BYTES` (1536 bytes), nothing enters `selected`. After the loop, the code reaches the pop-while (lines 948-956) which exits immediately since `selected` is empty, falling through to line 958 which returns only the omission bullet. A single dependency with a long title (easily possible) thus eliminates all concrete dependency identifiers and statuses from the prompt. The renderer should degrade by truncating long fields rather than dropping all actionable content.
